frameworks.name: GABAergic inhibition in orientation selectivity
frameworks.one_line: Cortical orientation tuning depends on GABA-mediated inhibitory network cooperation.

frameworks.name: gamma oscillations as a marker of inhibitory circuit function
frameworks.one_line: Stimulus-induced gamma frequency reflects inhibitory dynamics relevant to perception.

frameworks.name: individual differences psychophysics
frameworks.one_line: Links interindividual neurophysiological variability to behavioral thresholds.

core_question: Does individual variability in visual cortex GABA concentration and gamma oscillation frequency predict orientation discrimination performance in humans?
key_assumptions: MRS-measured resting GABA indexes functional inhibitory tone in V1
MEG stimulus-induced gamma frequency reflects local inhibitory circuit dynamics
Orientation discrimination thresholds index V1 orientation selectivity
manipulations: Across-subject measurement of V1 GABA (MRS) and gamma frequency (MEG)
key_findings: Behavioral oblique effect: oblique thresholds ~5x higher than vertical
Oblique thresholds negatively correlate with V1 GABA concentration
Oblique thresholds negatively correlate with gamma oscillation frequency
Gamma frequency positively correlates with V1 GABA concentration
interpretations: GABAergic inhibition is critical for orientation selectivity and behavior
GABAergic gamma oscillations play a key role in visual discrimination
